svg.title: Module lifecycle state machine driven by dev.sh
svg.desc: A module starts by tagging the git commit, then a contract test is written once with an optional golden harness branch. The module enters an iterate loop of write, review, and test, which can fail back to write up to three times or escalate to the frontier model if still failing. On pass, the module is finalised, queued, and reviewed by a human who either approves it or rejects it into a fix loop that re-enters iterate, with a hard stop after three rejections that sends the module down to revise the specification with the frontier model.

g.text: Refuses dirty tree or missing commit
